description: Graph comparison tests using the hierarchical pipeline (node_match_score, edge_existence_score, polyhedral_mode_score, geometric_distortion_score)

tests.description: SrTiO3 vs BaTiO3 (both cubic Pm-3m perovskite): should be nearly identical

assertions.description: Cubic perovskites with identical connectivity should score >= 0.90
Fingerprint distance should be low (<= 0.15) for isostructural pair

tests.description: SrTiO3 vs LaNiO3 R-3c (rhombohedral perovskite): same corner-sharing topology, distortion is geometric not topological

assertions.description: Cubic and rhombohedral perovskites share the same polyhedral topology — should score 1.0
Octahedral tilt distortion should lower angle similarity vs cubic (<= 0.90)
Fingerprint distance should be higher than isostructural pairs (>= 0.15)

tests.description: SrTiO3 (perovskite) vs TiFeO3 (ilmenite): different structural families

assertions.description: Corner-sharing perovskite vs face/edge-sharing ilmenite: few connections map at all
Polyhedral modes should not match between different families

tests.description: BaZrO3 vs SrTiO3: both cubic Pm-3m perovskite, different A and B site chemistry

assertions.description: Two cubic perovskites should score >= 0.95
Fingerprint distance <= 0.10 for identical-topology pair

tests.description: TiO2 vs SnO2: both rutile (P42/mnm) — different metal, identical topology

assertions.description: Isostructural rutile pair should score >= 0.95
Fingerprint distance <= 0.15 for isostructural pair

tests.description: MgO vs NaCl: both rocksalt (Fm-3m) — binary prototype structures

assertions.description: Isostructural rocksalt pair should score >= 0.95
Fingerprint distance <= 0.10 for nearly-ideal rocksalt pair

tests.description: Al2O3 vs Cr2O3: both R-3c corundum — chemical variant, identical connectivity

assertions.description: Isostructural corundum pair should score >= 0.90
Fingerprint distance <= 0.15 for corundum pair

tests.description: LaFeO3 vs GdFeO3: both Pbnm GdFeO3-type distorted perovskite

assertions.description: GdFeO3-type pair with strong Gd distortion — should score >= 0.75
Fingerprint distance should be moderate (<= 0.35) for same-type distorted pair

tests.description: Al2O3 corundum vs TiFeO3 ilmenite: same underlying face+edge-sharing topology, single-element vs ordered-cation variant

assertions.description: Corundum/ilmenite share face+edge topology — connections should exist >= 0.75
Same corundum/ilmenite wiring topology — mode score should be >= 0.75

tests.description: LaFeO3 GdFeO3-type vs SrTiO3 cubic perovskite: same corner-sharing connectivity but significant angular distortion from octahedral tilting

assertions.description: Related perovskites lower bound — still recognizably connected
Tilt distortion should prevent near-perfect match
Fingerprint distance should reflect structural difference (>= 0.10)

tests.description: NaCl rocksalt vs TiO2 rutile: different families — CN=3 O in rutile vs CN=6 in rocksalt

assertions.description: Different structural families: rocksalt vs rutile should score <= 0.55
Polyhedral modes should not match between different families

tests.description: MgAl2O4 spinel vs SrTiO3 perovskite: very different — spinel has mixed CN=4/6, perovskite CN=12/6

assertions.description: Spinel vs perovskite: completely different coordination environments should score <= 0.45
Polyhedral modes should not match between spinel and perovskite

tests.description: ReO3 (corner-sharing cubic) vs TiFeO3 (face/edge-sharing ilmenite): structurally unrelated

assertions.description: Corner-connected vs face/edge-sharing network: few connections should map
Polyhedral modes should not match between corner-only and face/edge-sharing
